companies.name: NVIDIA
companies.summary: VivaTech GTC Paris closed June 20 with Jensen Huang confirming more than 20 European AI factories in build-out and unveiling Mistral Compute — a sovereign AI GPU cloud platform built with Mistral AI for European customers. Foxconn showcased NVIDIA Vera Rubin NVL72 rack-scale systems and announced a co-manufacturing partnership with France's Bull. NVIDIA's ENPIRE system — released June 17 by the GEAR Lab — enables AI coding agents to autonomously run physical robot experiments with a 99% success rate, with no human in the loop between iterations. NVIDIA's FY2026 AI semiconductor revenue target remains approximately $56 billion; Vera Rubin CPUs are in production at Anthropic, OpenAI, and SpaceXAI.

items.headline: NVIDIA and Mistral AI unveiled Mistral Compute, a sovereign AI GPU cloud for Europe, at VivaTech
items.detail: NVIDIA and Mistral AI jointly announced Mistral Compute — a sovereign AI GPU cloud platform for European customers and governments — at VivaTech 2026, closing June 20. The platform gives European organizations access to high-performance AI infrastructure without relying on U.S. hyperscalers, built on NVIDIA hardware and Mistral's open models. Jensen Huang and Arthur Mensch presented the partnership as a flagship example of European AI sovereignty.

items.headline: NVIDIA confirmed 20+ European AI factories in build-out as VivaTech GTC Paris closed June 20
items.detail: Jensen Huang delivered a progress report at VivaTech on NVIDIA's European AI infrastructure commitments: more than 20 AI factories across Europe are in active build-out, including gigawatt-class gigafactories. Foxconn showcased NVIDIA Vera Rubin NVL72 rack-scale systems and announced a co-manufacturing partnership with France's Bull to assemble AI computers locally in Europe.

items.headline: NVIDIA ENPIRE: AI coding agents run autonomous physical robot experiments at 99% success rate
items.detail: NVIDIA's GEAR Lab (in collaboration with CMU and UC Berkeley) released ENPIRE — a closed-loop framework that hands the entire robotics research cycle to AI coding agents: resetting physical scenes, running hardware trials, verifying outcomes, and rewriting code with no human required between iterations. Agent teams achieved a 99% pass@8 success rate on demanding contact-rich tasks including seating a GPU into a motherboard slot and tying a zip tie. ENPIRE is the first documented system where frontier coding agents conduct 'physical autoresearch' on real hardware rather than simulation, with an open-source release planned.

companies.name: Alphabet / Google DeepMind
companies.category: Foundation models, search, cloud, and AI research
companies.summary: Demis Hassabis joined Anthropic's Dario Amodei at the G7 summit in Évian-les-Bains on June 17 to jointly call for a U.S.-led AI international coalition. The Gemini CLI to Antigravity CLI migration completed June 18 for individual users. Apple's reported $1 billion per year deal for Gemini to power a rebuilt Siri AI — launching September 2026 — is the most significant distribution milestone for Google DeepMind's commercial reach this cycle.

items.headline: Demis Hassabis and Dario Amodei jointly called for a U.S.-led AI coalition at the G7 summit
items.detail: Google DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis and Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei appeared together at the G7 summit in Évian-les-Bains on June 17 to jointly call for the United States to lead an international AI safety and governance coalition. The joint appearance — the first public collaboration between the two CEOs — came as Trump met separately with Amodei and signaled potential easing of the Fable 5 export restrictions.

items.headline: Gemini CLI migration to Antigravity CLI completed June 18 for individual users
items.detail: Google's transition from Gemini CLI to the Antigravity CLI platform completed on June 18 for Google AI Pro/Ultra individual and free users. Antigravity CLI, built in Go, supports multi-agent orchestration, background task execution, and Antigravity plugins replacing Gemini CLI's Agent Skills, Hooks, and Subagents capabilities. Enterprise Google Cloud Gemini Code Assist Standard/Enterprise customers retain legacy Gemini CLI access during a longer transition period.

items.headline: Apple's reported $1B/year Gemini deal to power Siri AI is Google DeepMind's largest known enterprise distribution contract
items.detail: Apple's reported $1 billion per year agreement for Google Gemini to power a rebuilt Siri AI — announced at WWDC 2026 (June 8) — represents Google DeepMind's largest known enterprise model distribution contract. Siri AI is expected to launch September 2026 on Google Cloud infrastructure powered by NVIDIA Blackwell chips, reaching an estimated 2.2 billion active Apple devices.

companies.name: Microsoft
companies.category: Cloud AI, copilots, and enterprise software
companies.summary: Microsoft is transitioning Copilot Cowork to usage-based pricing (billing began June 19) and exploring a Microsoft-hosted fine-tuned open-source model as a lower-cost alternative to Anthropic and OpenAI models powering the product. Microsoft 365 Business Standard with Copilot and Business Premium with Copilot become permanent commercial SKUs on July 1. Microsoft IQ (Work IQ + Web IQ) is now GA across GitHub Copilot, Microsoft Foundry, and Copilot Studio. New Agentic AI certifications AB-100 and AB-620 are entering the catalog.

items.headline: Microsoft Copilot Cowork moves to usage-based pricing; exploring open-source model alternatives to reduce API costs
items.detail: Microsoft is transitioning Copilot Cowork to usage-based pricing now that billing has begun (June 19), with frontline preview tenants receiving a grace period until July 1. Axios reported Microsoft is exploring hosting a fine-tuned version of DeepSeek or a comparable open-source model as a lower-cost foundation for some Copilot workloads, potentially reducing dependency on Anthropic and OpenAI API costs as agentic token consumption scales.

items.headline: Microsoft 365 Business Standard and Business Premium with Copilot become permanent SKUs July 1
items.detail: Effective July 1, 2026, Microsoft 365 Business Standard with Copilot and Microsoft 365 Business Premium with Copilot will transition to permanent commercial SKUs, formalizing Copilot's position in the core SMB and mid-market Microsoft 365 stack. A global price update accompanies the SKU formalization.

items.headline: Microsoft IQ is generally available; new Agentic AI certifications AB-100 and AB-620 entering catalog
items.detail: Microsoft IQ — combining Work IQ APIs (organizational context inside the M365 trust boundary) and Web IQ (real-time web grounding) — reached general availability across GitHub Copilot, Microsoft Foundry, and Copilot Studio as of June 16. Microsoft is simultaneously adding two new agent-focused certification paths: Agentic AI Business Solutions Architect (AB-100) and AI Agent Builder Associate (AB-620), while retiring some legacy Copilot Applied Skills certifications on June 30.

companies.name: OpenAI
companies.summary: GPT-5.6 remains in the June 22-28 launch window (~80-89% probability per prediction markets), with OpenAI's chief scientist describing it as a 'meaningful leap' featuring a 1.5 million token context window, stronger agentic workflows, and improved token efficiency. The Astral acquisition (uv Python package manager, ruff linter/formatter) announced June 19 is being integrated into the Codex ecosystem to cover the full software development lifecycle. OpenAI's confidential S-1 was filed June 8; CFO Sarah Friar has reportedly expressed concern about operational readiness, with a potential delay to 2027.

items.headline: GPT-5.6 launch window remains June 22-28 with 80-89% probability; 1.5M context window expected
items.detail: OpenAI's chief scientist described GPT-5.6 as a 'meaningful leap' with a 1.5 million token context window, improved agentic workflow performance, stronger UI generation, and better token efficiency — developed in approximately six weeks following GPT-5.5. Expected in Mini, standard, and Pro variants with an updated voice mode. Prediction markets assign 80-89% probability to a public release before June 30.

items.headline: OpenAI integrates Astral (uv, ruff) into Codex for full software development lifecycle participation
items.detail: Following the confirmed Astral acquisition (announced June 19), OpenAI is integrating uv (Python package manager) and ruff (Python linter/formatter) into the Codex ecosystem to evolve it from code generation into a full software development lifecycle participant — covering planning, tool execution, verification, and software maintenance. OpenAI stated it will continue supporting Astral's open-source products.

items.headline: OpenAI IPO: Confidential S-1 filed June 8; CFO may push listing to 2027
items.detail: OpenAI confirmed its confidential S-1 filing with the SEC on June 8, 2026, with Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley, and JPMorgan leading the offering. A September-November 2026 window has been discussed internally, but CFO Sarah Friar has reportedly expressed concerns about operational and compliance readiness, with a potential delay to 2027. CEO Sam Altman told staff he expects a listing within the next year.

companies.name: Anthropic
companies.summary: Day 8 of the Fable 5 and Mythos 5 global suspension, with June 20 marking the Fable 5 subscriber refund cutoff. Trump told Axios after his G7 meeting with CEO Dario Amodei that he no longer views Anthropic as a national security threat — the strongest positive U.S. government signal since the June 12 suspension — but no executive order or Commerce Department reversal has been issued as of June 20. The Globe and Mail reported active deal-making between Anthropic and Trump administration officials toward a joint risk framework. Beginning June 23, Fable 5 moves to a credit-based model ($10/$50 per million tokens) rather than subscription access. Revenue run rate has surpassed $30 billion. Amodei and Google DeepMind's Demis Hassabis jointly called for a U.S.-led AI coalition at G7.

items.headline: Trump told Axios he no longer views Anthropic as a national security threat after G7 meeting with Amodei
items.detail: In a post-G7 interview with Axios, President Trump said he no longer views Anthropic as a national security threat after a closed-door lunch with CEO Dario Amodei in Évian-les-Bains on June 17 — the strongest positive government signal since the June 12 Fable 5/Mythos 5 suspension began. No executive order or Commerce Department reversal has been issued as of June 20. The Globe and Mail reported active deal-making between Anthropic and Trump administration officials toward a framework for restoring the models.

items.headline: June 20 Fable 5 refund cutoff: day 8 of suspension; credit-based access model starts June 23
items.detail: The June 20 subscriber refund cutoff has arrived for users who paid for Fable 5 access between June 9-14, with both Fable 5 and Mythos 5 still offline globally. The free trial window closes June 22. Beginning June 23, Fable 5 transitions to a credit-based access model priced at $10/million input tokens and $50/million output tokens — roughly half the cost of Claude Mythos Preview but a significant premium over standard Claude models. Anthropic intends to restore Fable 5 as a standard subscription feature once capacity allows. Security experts previously called the White House's reported zero-jailbreak precondition technically impossible for any frontier model. Anthropic's revenue run rate has surpassed $30 billion, suggesting other Claude models remain commercially active.

items.headline: Amodei and Google DeepMind CEO jointly called for U.S.-led AI coalition at G7
items.detail: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei and Google DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis made a joint appearance at the G7 summit on June 17, together calling for the United States to lead an international coalition to set AI safety and governance standards. The joint statement represents a coordinated industry response to the Fable 5 export control episode and broader AI governance uncertainty.

items.headline: Anthropic signed enterprise partnerships with DXC Technology and Tata Consultancy Services
items.detail: Anthropic continued enterprise expansion during the Fable 5 suspension, signing new deployment partnerships with DXC Technology — covering banking, airline, and regulated-industry systems — and Tata Consultancy Services (TCS), reaching TCS's 600,000-plus employee base. Both partnerships rely on Claude models outside of Fable 5 and Mythos 5, reinforcing that non-flagged models remain commercially active.

companies.name: Meta
companies.category: Open models, consumer AI, and infrastructure
companies.summary: Meta launched Creator Assistant on Facebook with AI-powered content performance analysis and growth recommendations. Meta is testing a 'Meta One' subscription umbrella expected to unify Facebook Plus, Instagram Plus, WhatsApp Plus, and AI product subscriptions. Meta's AI Gateway internal budget-cap platform — launched after employees consumed 73.7 trillion tokens in 30 days — remains the most operationally significant recent development as agentic token costs scale. 2026 capex guidance of $115-135 billion is unchanged.

items.headline: Meta launched Creator Assistant on Facebook with AI-powered audience and growth recommendations
items.detail: Meta launched Creator Assistant on Facebook — an AI tool that analyzes content performance, audience behavior patterns, and growth goals to surface personalized recommendations for creators. The tool is embedded directly in Facebook's creator hub and reflects Meta's push to use AI to deepen creator retention and monetization on the platform.

items.headline: Meta testing 'Meta One' subscription umbrella to unify Facebook, Instagram, WhatsApp, and AI subscriptions
items.detail: Meta is testing a 'Meta One' subscription umbrella brand designed to unify Facebook Plus, Instagram Plus, WhatsApp Plus, and forthcoming AI product subscriptions for creators and businesses into a single membership tier. The move is part of Meta's push to build sustainable subscription revenue alongside advertising as AI infrastructure costs scale.

items.headline: Meta AI Mode on Facebook synthesizes answers from public posts across Groups and Reels
items.detail: Meta launched AI Mode on Facebook on June 15, letting users ask plain-language questions and receive synthesized answers drawn from public posts, Groups, and Reels across the platform. The feature uses Meta AI to surface community-sourced knowledge for Meta's 3.2 billion daily active users, positioning Facebook as an AI-powered information layer alongside traditional social feeds.

companies.name: Amazon / AWS
companies.summary: AWS Summit New York (June 17-20) concluded with EC2 G7 instances featuring NVIDIA RTX PRO 4500 Blackwell GPUs (up to 4.6x AI inference performance vs. prior generation), Amazon Bedrock AgentCore GA, AWS Continuum (AI-native security), and AWS Context (knowledge graph for agents) as the flagship releases. Amazon Bedrock now hosts frontier models from Anthropic, OpenAI, and xAI under unified enterprise governance — positioning Bedrock as the enterprise multi-model control plane.

items.headline: AWS launched EC2 G7 instances with NVIDIA RTX PRO 4500 Blackwell, delivering 4.6x AI inference performance
items.detail: Amazon launched EC2 G7 instances accelerated by NVIDIA RTX PRO 4500 Blackwell Server Edition GPUs at AWS Summit New York — making AWS the first major cloud provider to support this GPU. G7 instances deliver up to 4.6x AI inference performance and 2.1x graphics performance compared to prior G7 instances, expanding AWS's GPU catalog for AI inference, 3D visualization, and high-performance workloads.

items.headline: AWS Summit New York concluded with AgentCore GA, AWS Continuum, and AWS Context as flagship enterprise releases
items.detail: AWS Summit New York (June 17-20) closed with Amazon Bedrock AgentCore general availability, AWS Continuum (AI-native security for agentic workloads), and AWS Context (knowledge graph service for agents) as flagship releases. AgentCore incorporates Managed Knowledge Base with Smart Parsing, fully managed Web Search, Agent Optimization with A/B testing and intent analytics, and Bedrock Guardrails for prompt injection detection.

items.headline: Amazon Bedrock is now the enterprise multi-model control plane hosting Anthropic, OpenAI, and xAI frontier models
items.detail: Following AgentCore GA, Grok 4.3 on Bedrock (June 15), and OpenAI frontier models/Codex (June 1), Amazon Bedrock has become the enterprise control plane where companies access Claude, GPT-5.5, and Grok under unified AWS governance, compliance, billing, and security workflows — with AgentCore providing the runtime for multi-model agentic workflows.

companies.name: xAI
companies.summary: SpaceX (SPCX) is trading approximately $210-220, up roughly 56% from its $135 IPO price since the June 12 debut — still pulling back from the June 16 all-time high of $225.64. The $60B all-stock Anysphere (Cursor AI) acquisition confirmed via SEC Form 8-K is expected to close Q3 2026. Colossus 1 operates 220,000 H100s with 300 MW of capacity. Grok launched as an AI shopping assistant inside Gopuff; native Grok availability on Databricks Agent Bricks was announced at the Data + AI Summit June 18.

items.headline: SPCX trading ~$210-220, up ~56% from IPO price; $60B Cursor acquisition expected to close Q3 2026
items.detail: SpaceX (SPCX) continues trading around $210-220, up approximately 56% from its June 12 IPO price of $135, with an implied valuation approaching $1.85 trillion. The $60B all-stock acquisition of Anysphere (Cursor AI), confirmed via SEC Form 8-K, is expected to receive regulatory clearance and close in Q3 2026. Cursor will be integrated into SpaceXAI's developer platform alongside Grok, with Colossus 1's 220,000 H100s and 300 MW providing shared infrastructure.

items.headline: Grok launched as AI shopping assistant in Gopuff app; now natively available on Databricks Agent Bricks
items.detail: xAI launched Grok as the AI engine inside Gopuff's new 'Go' shopping assistant in the US (iOS and Android), using Grok's text, audio, and image models for personalized cart building and a visual shopping feed. Separately, SpaceXAI announced native Grok availability on Databricks Agent Bricks at the Data + AI Summit (June 18), giving enterprise data teams access to Grok for agentic data workflows inside Databricks' lakehouse platform.

items.headline: xAI appointed Jack Galabedian from SpaceX Starlink to lead Grok model training team
items.detail: xAI announced the appointment of Jack Galabedian — a former SpaceX Starlink project engineer — to lead the Grok model training team as part of a broader management restructure following the SpaceX acquisition in February 2026. The leadership change signals deeper integration of SpaceX operational culture into the AI model development organization.

companies.name: Broadcom
companies.category: AI networking, custom silicon, and infrastructure software
companies.summary: Broadcom (AVGO) goes ex-dividend June 22 with a $0.65 quarterly cash dividend payable June 30. Q2 FY2026 AI semiconductor revenue hit $10.8 billion — up 143% year-over-year — driven by custom TPU demand and broader AI accelerator adoption. Broadcom's FY2026 AI semiconductor revenue target remains approximately $56 billion; Q3 guidance is $16 billion. Tomahawk 6 (Davisson), the industry's first 102.4 Tbps Ethernet switch with co-packaged optics, is the most significant new product.

items.headline: Broadcom goes ex-dividend June 22; Q2 AI revenue hit $10.8B up 143% year-over-year
items.detail: Broadcom (AVGO) has an ex-dividend date of June 22, 2026, with a $0.65 per share quarterly cash dividend payable June 30. Q2 FY2026 AI semiconductor revenue reached $10.8 billion — up 143% year-over-year — on record quarterly revenue, operating profit, and free cash flow. Broadcom guided Q3 AI semiconductor revenue to approximately $16 billion.

items.headline: Broadcom FY2026 AI target is $56B; FY2027 target exceeds $100B across six custom-silicon customers
items.detail: Broadcom's FY2026 AI semiconductor revenue target of approximately $56 billion is unchanged, with Q3 guidance of $16 billion as the current benchmark. Broadcom's $100+ billion FY2027 target is driven by its six core custom-silicon customers — including Anthropic, Google, and Meta — for whom it is developing next-generation TPU and ASIC accelerators under multi-year agreements.

items.headline: Broadcom Tomahawk 6 (Davisson) — industry's first 102.4 Tbps Ethernet switch with co-packaged optics
items.detail: The Tomahawk 6 (codename Davisson), unveiled in mid-June 2026, is the industry's first 102.4 Tbps Ethernet switch silicon with co-packaged optics, addressing the bandwidth demands of AI data centers where conventional pluggable optics are insufficient at hyperscale. JPMorgan maintains an 'aggressive buy' rating on AVGO; 48 analysts hold a 'Strong Buy' consensus with a $522 12-month price target.

companies.name: Palantir
companies.summary: Palantir AIP is now available on Google Cloud Marketplace with BigQuery and Gemini integrations, expanding cloud distribution beyond AWS and Azure. AIPCon 10 (June 4) featured new enterprise deployments including Kirkland & Ellis, McCarthy Building Companies, and GNP Seguros. PLTR slipped 1.7% to $128.47 as software names underperformed the mid-June semiconductor rally. Maven Smart System retains DoD program-of-record status with multi-year protected funding; Trump's June 20 Fable 5 signal has not yet translated into a formal Pentagon-Anthropic supply chain reversal.

items.headline: Palantir AIP is now available on Google Cloud Marketplace with BigQuery and Gemini integrations
items.detail: Palantir's AIP (Artificial Intelligence Platform) is now available on Google Cloud Marketplace, with integrations connecting Google BigQuery for data access and Gemini models for AI capabilities inside AIP workflows. The listing expands Palantir's addressable market to Google Cloud enterprise customers and diversifies cloud distribution beyond AWS and Azure.

items.headline: Palantir AIPCon 10 featured new enterprise AI deployments with Kirkland & Ellis and McCarthy Building Companies
items.detail: Palantir's AIPCon 10 conference (June 4) featured new enterprise AI deployment agreements including Kirkland & Ellis (legal sector), McCarthy Building Companies (construction AI operating system), and GNP Seguros (commercial insurance). The agreements reflect AIP's continued expansion from defense and intelligence into regulated commercial verticals.

items.headline: Maven Smart System retains DoD program-of-record status as Pentagon-Anthropic dispute remains formally unresolved
items.detail: Palantir's Maven Smart System retains its DoD program-of-record designation with multi-year protected funding across five military branches. Trump's June 20 signal that Anthropic is no longer viewed as a national security threat has not yet produced a formal Commerce Department reversal or restoration of Pentagon-Anthropic supply chain access. Claude remains integrated in Maven for eligible workflows pending resolution.

companies.name: Apple
companies.summary: Apple's reported $1 billion per year Google Gemini deal to power a rebuilt Siri AI — launching September 2026 — is the defining Apple AI story, with the iOS 27 Extensions framework (allowing ChatGPT, Claude, or Gemini inside Siri) remaining disabled. Bloomberg reports OpenAI is weighing a breach-of-contract notice. Intel has begun preliminary small-scale chip testing for Apple following the Trump manufacturing push, though no finalized production deal has been announced. Apple reportedly also considered acquiring Mistral AI and Perplexity AI.

items.headline: Apple's reported $1B/year Gemini deal powers rebuilt Siri AI launching September 2026 on NVIDIA cloud
items.detail: Apple reportedly struck a $1 billion per year deal for Google Gemini to power a rebuilt Siri AI — announced at WWDC 2026 (June 8) — to launch in September 2026. Siri AI will run on Google Cloud infrastructure powered by NVIDIA Blackwell chips, delivering persistent memory, on-screen awareness, and Visual Intelligence. The EU launch is delayed pending unresolved Digital Markets Act regulatory talks.

items.headline: OpenAI weighing breach-of-contract notice as iOS 27 Extensions framework stays disabled
items.detail: Bloomberg reports OpenAI is weighing a breach-of-contract notice against Apple over perceived lack of prime ChatGPT placement in Siri following the Google Gemini deal. The iOS 27 developer beta contains a hidden Extensions framework that would allow users to choose ChatGPT, Claude, or Gemini inside Siri, but Apple has disabled it pending DMA regulatory clarity and resolution with OpenAI.

items.headline: Intel began preliminary chip testing for Apple; Apple reportedly considered acquiring Mistral AI and Perplexity
items.detail: Intel began small-scale preliminary testing of chip fabrication for iPhone, iPad, and Mac following the Trump-backed manufacturing push; TSMC remains Apple's exclusive supplier for current product lines. Separately, Sherwood News reported Apple considered acquiring Mistral AI and Perplexity AI — signaling appetite for AI strategy options beyond the existing OpenAI and Google partnerships.

companies.name: AMD
companies.category: AI accelerators, CPUs, and data center silicon
companies.summary: AMD CEO Lisa Su confirmed MI450 GPU samples are shipping to lead customers including OpenAI and Meta; AMD denied MI455X delay reports, stating Helios rack systems remain on target for H2 2026. The MEXT acquisition (completed June 15) and the Rackspace 30 MW AMD AI Compute deployment (signed June 16) are the most recent operational milestones. AMD's Advancing AI 2026 event on July 23 is the next major disclosure milestone. AMD reports Q2 2026 results August 4.

items.headline: AMD confirmed MI450 sampling to OpenAI and Meta; Helios on target for H2 2026 despite delay reports
items.detail: AMD CEO Lisa Su confirmed MI450 GPUs are sampling to lead customers including OpenAI and Meta. AMD denied reports of MI455X delays, stating Helios rack systems — AMD's NVIDIA Vera Rubin competitor — remain on target for H2 2026 production. MI450 delivers up to 40 PFLOPs FP4 performance. Early customer engagement on the successor MI500 architecture is also active.

items.headline: AMD completed MEXT acquisition and signed Rackspace 30 MW AI Compute deployment
items.detail: AMD completed the acquisition of MEXT on June 15 — bringing predictive memory technology that makes flash storage behave like DRAM for AI workloads. On June 16, AMD signed a definitive agreement with Rackspace Technology for a phased 30 MW AMD AI Compute deployment, adding hyperscale cloud capacity for MI450-class workloads.

items.headline: AMD Advancing AI 2026 event set for July 23 — key milestone for MI400 series and OpenAI/Meta deployment disclosures
items.detail: AMD's Advancing AI 2026 event on July 23 is the next major milestone for detailed MI400 series disclosures, including MI450 production timelines, competitive benchmarks, pricing, and MI500 roadmap visibility. This event is expected to include deployment updates for the combined 12 GW of committed AMD GPU deployments from OpenAI (6 GW) and Meta (6 GW). AMD reports Q2 2026 financial results on August 4.

companies.name: Perplexity AI
companies.summary: Perplexity CEO Aravind Srinivas confirmed the 2028 IPO target remains unchanged despite Anthropic and OpenAI moving toward public markets. ARR reached $500 million (April 2026, 335% YoY growth), with 100M+ monthly active users and a $20 billion valuation from the May 2026 round. Apple reportedly considered acquiring Perplexity alongside Mistral AI (Sherwood News, June 19) — the first notable M&A signal for the company. The 'Computer' agentic tool is live as native side panels in Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook, and Teams.

items.headline: Perplexity CEO confirmed 2028 IPO target; $500M ARR, 100M+ MAU, $20B valuation
items.detail: CEO Aravind Srinivas confirmed the 2028 IPO target as a deliberate strategy on June 9, 2026, stating no need to rush public markets despite Anthropic and OpenAI S-1 filings. ARR reached $500 million in April 2026 (335% year-over-year growth), with 100M+ monthly active users and a $20B valuation from the May 2026 fundraising round.

items.headline: Apple reportedly considered acquiring Perplexity alongside Mistral AI — CEO has not confirmed discussions
items.detail: Sherwood News reported on June 19 that Apple considered acquiring Perplexity AI alongside Mistral AI as part of Apple's exploration of AI strategy options beyond its existing OpenAI partnership. CEO Aravind Srinivas has publicly stated a preference for an independent 2028 IPO and has not confirmed acquisition discussions. Perplexity's $20B valuation is the most relevant current benchmark for any potential acquisition price.

items.headline: Comet browser at worldwide availability; Perplexity Computer live as native M365 side panels
items.detail: Perplexity's Comet AI browser is globally available and reached the #3 App Store position within 48 hours of its iOS launch. The 'Computer' agentic tool expanded into native side panels in Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook, and Teams — positioning Perplexity as a workplace AI layer competing directly alongside Microsoft Copilot inside Microsoft 365.

companies.name: CoreWeave
companies.summary: CoreWeave (CRWV) is two days away from Nasdaq-100 inclusion before market open June 22, with Russell 3000 inclusion June 27 — both creating structural passive-fund buying pressure. The $3.55B high-yield bond deal completed this week broadens capital market access. The contracted revenue backlog is approaching ~$131 billion (up ~$32B since end-Q1 per analyst estimates). The 2026 capex floor was raised to $31 billion for Vera Rubin NVL72 buildout serving Meta, Anthropic, and OpenAI.

items.headline: CoreWeave joins Nasdaq-100 before market open June 22, triggering mandatory passive-fund buying
items.detail: CoreWeave (CRWV) officially joins the Nasdaq-100 Index prior to market open June 22 — just 15 months post-IPO — triggering mandatory buying from QQQ and Nasdaq-100 tracking funds. CRWV surged 9.67% on the announcement. The company also joins the Russell 3000 on June 27. Both inclusions create structural buying pressure as passive and benchmark-tracking funds acquire CRWV shares.

items.headline: CoreWeave completed $3.55B high-yield bond deal; backlog approaching $131B; 2026 capex floor raised to $31B
items.detail: CoreWeave completed a $3.55 billion high-yield bond deal, broadening capital market access ahead of Nasdaq-100 inclusion. Analyst estimates put the contracted revenue backlog at approximately $131 billion, up roughly $32 billion since reporting $99.4 billion at end-Q1 2026. The 2026 capex floor was raised to $31 billion to accommodate Vera Rubin NVL72 commitments from Meta, Anthropic, and OpenAI.

items.headline: CoreWeave Q1 2026 revenue hit $2.1 billion (+112% YoY); Vera Rubin NVL72 bring-up completed June 1
items.detail: CoreWeave reported Q1 2026 revenue of $2.1 billion, up 112% year-over-year, in its first public earnings report. CoreWeave completed the industry-first Vera Rubin NVL72 system bring-up on June 1, enabling next-generation AI compute density for Meta, Anthropic, and OpenAI workloads from its expanded data center fleet.

companies.name: Mistral AI
companies.summary: Mistral AI confirmed a $3.5 billion raise at a €20 billion valuation — nearly doubling its prior €11.7B September 2025 Series C — and disclosed its Bruyères-le-Châtel data center in France is live with 18,000 NVIDIA GB200 systems. At VivaTech (June 17-20), Mistral and NVIDIA unveiled Mistral Compute, a sovereign AI GPU cloud for European customers. CEO Arthur Mensch confirmed 1,000 employees and a €1 billion 2026 revenue target. Apple reportedly considered an acquisition alongside Perplexity.

items.headline: Mistral confirmed $3.5B raise at €20B valuation; Bruyères-le-Châtel data center live with 18,000 GB200s
items.detail: Mistral AI confirmed a $3.5 billion fundraise at a €20 billion valuation — nearly doubling its prior €11.7 billion September 2025 Series C. At VivaTech, Mistral disclosed that its Bruyères-le-Châtel data center in France is operational with 18,000 NVIDIA GB200 systems, the first node in a planned 200 MW European compute roadmap by 2027. CEO Arthur Mensch confirmed 1,000 employees and a €1 billion 2026 revenue target.

items.headline: NVIDIA and Mistral unveiled Mistral Compute — a sovereign AI GPU cloud for European customers — at VivaTech
items.detail: NVIDIA and Mistral AI jointly announced Mistral Compute at VivaTech 2026 — a sovereign AI GPU cloud platform for European customers and governments built on NVIDIA hardware and Mistral's open models. Jensen Huang highlighted Mistral Compute as a key example of European AI sovereignty infrastructure and reaffirmed Mistral as the flagship of France's sovereign AI strategy.

items.headline: CEO Mensch positions Mistral as open-source sovereignty alternative amid Anthropic Fable ban
items.detail: CEO Arthur Mensch is actively leveraging European concern over the Anthropic Fable/Mythos export suspension to position Mistral as the open-source alternative that governments cannot revoke — stating 'We exist to make sure that everyone gets access to the best AI systems, outside of centralised control exercised by states or corporations.' Apple reportedly considered acquiring Mistral alongside Perplexity, per Sherwood News; Mensch has not confirmed acquisition discussions.

companies.name: Tesla
companies.category: Autonomous vehicles, robotics, and applied AI
companies.summary: An EPA filing dated June 19 revealed the full Tesla Cybercab specifications: 47.6 kWh battery, 163 kW (219 hp) front-wheel-drive permanent magnet motor, 3,113 lb curb weight, and 418 miles of unadjusted combined range (~293 miles adjusted). Giga Texas Cybercab production commenced May 29. The robotaxi service covers Austin, Dallas, Houston, and the SF Bay Area. Optimus Gen 3 is in mass production at Fremont; V3 full-body targets summer 2026. FSD v14.3 reduces inference latency 20%.

items.headline: EPA filing revealed full Cybercab specs: 47.6 kWh battery, 418-mile unadjusted range, Giga Texas production underway
items.detail: An EPA filing dated June 19, 2026 revealed complete Tesla Cybercab vehicle specifications: 47.6 kWh battery pack, 163 kW (219 hp) front-wheel-drive permanent magnet motor, 3,113 lb curb weight, and 418 miles of unadjusted combined range (approximately 293 miles adjusted, consistent with Tesla's 'close to 300 miles' claim). Giga Texas production of Cybercabs commenced May 29, 2026.

items.headline: Cybercab robotaxi service covers Austin, Dallas, Houston, and SF Bay Area; expansion continues
items.detail: Tesla's unsupervised Cybercab service covers the full Austin metro area, Dallas, and Houston as of early June 2026, with the SF Bay Area pilot also active. Expansion continues as Cybercabs come off the Giga Texas production line. Production Cybercab specs — two-passenger, butterfly doors, no door handles or rear window, hatchback cargo configuration — became public via the June 19 EPA filing.

items.headline: Optimus Gen 3 in mass production at Fremont; V3 full-body targeting summer 2026; FSD v14.3 cuts latency 20%
items.detail: Tesla's Optimus Gen 3 humanoid robot entered mass production at Fremont in January 2026, with V3 full-body production targeting summer 2026. A dedicated Optimus factory is under construction at Giga Texas North Campus. FSD (Supervised) v14.3 launched in April 2026 with a rewritten AI compiler reducing inference latency by up to 20% and an enhanced neural network vision encoder for low-visibility scenarios.

ipoTracker.status: Confirmed filing; Fable/Mythos ban enters day 8; Trump signals possible easing after G7 meeting
ipoTracker.process: Anthropic confirmed its confidential draft Form S-1 submission to the SEC on June 1, 2026, after closing a $65 billion Series H at a ~$965 billion post-money valuation (May 28). An October 2026 listing window is widely reported. As of June 20, Fable 5 and Mythos 5 remain offline globally (day 8 of suspension). Trump told Axios after a G7 meeting with CEO Dario Amodei that he no longer views Anthropic as a national security threat — the strongest positive government signal since the ban began. No executive order or formal reversal has been issued. June 20 is the Fable 5 subscriber refund cutoff; the free trial window closes June 22. Anthropic's revenue run rate has surpassed $30 billion, suggesting other Claude models remain commercially active. Fortune identified government power to suspend model access as a primary IPO risk.
ipoTracker.note: Trump's post-G7 Axios statement is the most positive government signal yet. Watch June 22 (trial window close) as the next key date. No formal reversal means the IPO risk narrative persists until a Commerce Department action or executive order is issued.

latestArticle.title: Trump told Axios he no longer views Anthropic as a national security threat after G7 meeting

ipoTracker.status: Confirmed SEC filing June 8; CFO may push to 2027; GPT-5.6 expected June 22-28
ipoTracker.process: OpenAI confirmed its confidential S-1 filing with the SEC on June 8, 2026. Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley, and JPMorgan are leading the offering. CEO Sam Altman told staff he expects a listing within the next year; however, CFO Sarah Friar has reportedly expressed concern about operational and compliance readiness, with a potential delay to 2027. An internal September–November 2026 window has been discussed but no date is set. GPT-5.6 is expected in the June 22-28 window (80-89% probability per prediction markets), which could serve as a pre-IPO product catalyst.
ipoTracker.note: CFO pushback on 2026 timing is the most significant new IPO risk signal. GPT-5.6's expected launch in the next 2-8 days could stabilize pre-IPO product momentum.

latestArticle.title: OpenAI's CFO reportedly wants to delay the IPO from 2026 to 2027

ipoTracker.status: SPCX trading ~$210-220; up ~56% from $135 IPO price; $60B Cursor acquisition pending Q3 close
ipoTracker.process: SpaceX priced at $135/share on June 11, raising $75 billion at a $1.75 trillion valuation in the largest IPO ever recorded. Shares peaked at $225.64 on June 16, trading around $210-220 on June 20 — still up roughly 56% from the IPO price in eight trading days. SpaceX confirmed via SEC Form 8-K the $60B all-stock acquisition of Anysphere (Cursor AI); pending regulatory and shareholder approval, expected to close Q3 2026. xAI was folded into SpaceX as the SpaceXAI division in February 2026; all Grok/AI exposure trades via ticker SPCX.
ipoTracker.confidence: Confirmed; public company as of June 12, 2026
ipoTracker.note: The $60B Cursor 8-K is confirmed. Grok's expanding enterprise distribution (Gopuff, Databricks) are primary near-term product catalysts. Q3 2026 is the expected Cursor acquisition close timeline.

Amazon confirmed Claude models (outside Fable 5 and Mythos 5) remain available to AWS customers.
